summary: Anomaly Visualization Layer with Recharts sparkline charts - Backend API 100% PASS (10/10 tests), Frontend UI verified with 9 devices and 81 incidents. MetricTrendsCard shows 'Insufficient data' for metrics with < 2 data points in the 24h window (expected behavior). All expand/collapse interactions work correctly.

backend.test_metric_trends_returns_correct_struc: PASS - device_id, window_minutes, bucket_seconds, total_points, points fields present
backend.test_metric_trends_point_structure: PASS - timestamp, battery_level, signal_strength, battery_score, signal_score, combined_score, samples fields present
backend.test_metric_trends_invalid_device_id: PASS - returns 200 with 0 points for non-existent device

frontend.device_row_click_expands: PASS - MetricTrendsCard appears on row click
frontend.device_row_click_collapses: PASS - MetricTrendsCard hides on second click
frontend.metric_trends_card_structure: PASS - Shows 'METRIC TRENDS (1D)' header with 3 sparkline sections
frontend.insufficient_data_handling: PASS - Shows 'Insufficient data' for metrics with < 2 points
frontend.sparkline_icons_present: PASS - Battery, Wifi, AlertTriangle icons displayed
frontend.anomaly_intelligence_sections: PASS - Battery, Signal, Combined anomaly sections present
frontend.incident_metric_trends: INFO - Not displayed (expected when device lacks 60min telemetry)
frontend.filter_elements_present: PASS - Search, status filter, severity filter all work

critical_code_review_comments: Backend endpoint correctly buckets time-series data with max(30, total_seconds // 120) formula
Backend limits to 120 points maximum with proper downsampling
Frontend MetricSparkline shows 'Insufficient data' when chartData.length < 2 (correct UX)
Frontend falls back from *_score to raw values (battery_level, signal_strength) appropriately
IncidentMetricTrends uses 60min window - may show nothing for old test data (expected)
MetricTrendsCard uses 1440min (24h) window on Device Health page
All data-testid attributes properly applied for testability
seed_data_creation: None - used existing test devices DEV-001 (8pts/7d), DEV-002 (6pts/7d)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Anomaly Visualization Layer fully tested. Backend GET /api/operator/devices/{device_id}/metric-trends returns time-series data bucketed for sparklines. Uses window_minutes parameter (5-10080). Returns device_id, window_minutes, bucket_seconds, total_points, points array. Each point has timestamp, battery_level, signal_strength, battery_score, signal_score, combined_score, samples. Frontend MetricSparkline component renders Recharts AreaChart with gradient fills. Shows 'Insufficient data' if < 2 points. Device Health page uses 1440min window, Incident page uses 60min window. Test data is sparse - most devices show 'Insufficient data' in 24h window but have data in 7d window.

notes.sparse_test_data: DEV-001 has 8 points over 7 days - only 0-1 points fall within 24h window causing 'Insufficient data' display
notes.expected_behavior: MetricSparkline correctly shows 'Insufficient data' when chartData.length < 2 after filtering nulls
